key_insight: BiMU facilitates active continual learning in Bayesian binary neural networks while effectively managing uncertainty and plasticity.
engineering_relevance: This approach is crucial for developing resilient edge systems that adapt to changing conditions with limited computational resources.
method: Balances stability and plasticity with a bounded-memory variational objective.
Utilizes a data term and controlled relaxation toward the prior.
Employs an uncertainty-dependent step size to maintain informative uncertainty.
evidence_signal: BiMU shows sustained learning and improved out-of-distribution detection across various tasks, including 1000-tasks Permuted-MNIST.
watch_item: The approach may require careful tuning of parameters to optimize performance under different operational conditions.
paper_title: Active Continual Learning with Metaplastic Binary Bayesian Neural Networks
